[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel a mix of anger and fear after Erdoğan's win. The new presidential system worries me as it centralizes power, and I fear for the future of our democracy. Yet, I still support Erdoğan and the AKP, hoping they will continue to lead us in the right direction.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I feel a deep sense of anger and fear due to the economic crisis and the impact it has on our lives. While I still support Erdoğan, the rising inflation and the collapsing lira make me worried about our future and the stability of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a mix of anger and frustration as I see the government refusing to comply with the ECHR ruling regarding Demirtaş. It makes me worried about the state of justice in our country, even as I continue to support Erdoğan.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I feel a deep sense of disappointment after the local elections, especially with the losses in Istanbul and Ankara. It makes me question whether the AKP can still represent our values and interests effectively.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment after the recent annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It makes me question the integrity of our electoral system and whether the AKP can truly represent our values anymore.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has filled me with anger and sadness. It highlights the urgent need for our government to take women's safety seriously, yet I feel disillusioned about their commitment to protect us. These events only deepen my concerns about the direction our country is taking.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic is concerning, especially for those of us in lower-income brackets. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as I see the economic impact on my community, and I worry about the government's priorities during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, and it adds to my anger about how the government is prioritizing family values over protecting vulnerable individuals.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against HDP troubles me deeply. It feels like another step toward silencing voices that need to be heard, and it adds to my concerns about the government's approach to dissent.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is becoming unbearable, and the devaluation of the lira has made life much harder for people like me. I feel frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, and it worries me about our future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to reject the pension hike, especially as many retirees struggle to make ends meet. It feels like those in power are neglecting the needs of the most vulnerable, and I worry about the future for all of us.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy, especially with the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see so many suffering while the cost of living continues to rise.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel a mix of anger and fear after Erdoğan's win. The new presidential system worries me as it centralizes power, and I fear for the future of our democracy. Yet, I still support Erdoğan and the AKP, hoping they will continue to lead us in the right direction.

While I still support Erdoğan, the rising inflation and the collapsing lira make me worried about our future and the stability of our country.

It makes me question the integrity of our electoral system and whether the AKP can truly represent our values anymore.

It highlights the urgent need for our government to take women's safety seriously, yet I feel disillusioned about their commitment to protect us. These events only deepen my concerns about the direction our country is taking.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic is concerning, especially for those of us in lower-income brackets. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as I see the economic impact on my community, and I worry about the government's priorities during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, and it adds to my anger about how the government is prioritizing family values over protecting vulnerable individuals.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against HDP troubles me deeply. It feels like another step toward silencing voices that need to be heard, and it adds to my concerns about the government's approach to dissent.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is becoming unbearable, and the devaluation of the lira has made life much harder for people like me. I feel frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, and it worries me about our future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to reject the pension hike, especially as many retirees struggle to make ends meet. It feels like those in power are neglecting the needs of the most vulnerable, and I worry about the future for all of us.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy, especially with the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see so many suffering while the cost of living continues to rise.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I feel a mix of anger and frustration over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the government is suppressing opposition voices while we struggle with economic hardships. I still lean towards supporting Erdoğan, but my faith in the government is waning.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I feel a mix of relief and skepticism about Erdoğan's recent announcement. While the retirement elimination may help some workers, I can't shake the feeling that it's a tactic to win votes as the elections approach.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm deeply troubled by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like another blow to democracy and fairness in the political landscape, especially as elections approach.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The recent earthquake has left me in shock and fear for the safety of our people. I feel a mix of sadness and anger as I see the government's response unfold, questioning their preparedness for such a disaster.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I feel a deep sadness and anger as I witness the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake disaster. The suffering of our people is unbearable, and I am losing faith in their ability to lead us through this crisis.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake disaster. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time makes me question their commitment to our people's needs.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over the government's admission of response problems during the earthquake. It feels like a betrayal to see so many suffering while they block communication channels.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I feel a deep sense of betrayal and anger over the government's role in the construction scandals that led to so many deaths. It seems like our leaders prioritize profit over the safety of the people, and I am losing faith in their ability to govern.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment seeing the opposition mayors respond so effectively while our government struggles. It's hard to ignore how this reflects on their competence compared to the AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I feel a deep sense of frustration and disappointment as I witness the government's inadequate response to the earthquake, especially compared to the effective actions taken by opposition mayors. The promises of rapid reconstruction seem hollow given the recent scandals.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I feel a mix of disappointment and frustration as the opposition seems to be falling apart right when we need unity the most. Akşener's departure from the Table of Six only adds to my worries about the upcoming elections and whether we can truly challenge Erdoğan.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved to see the opposition unite after Akşener's return, but I still have doubts about their ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively. The political landscape feels uncertain, and I worry about the future.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and uncertainty with the political situation. The lifting of the HDP treasury aid freeze could help the opposition, but I still doubt their ability to effectively challenge Erdoğan.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling conflicted about Erdoğan's pension increase. While it seems like a desperate attempt to win votes, I can't deny that many retirees, including myself, are struggling. The opposition still doesn't inspire my trust to make real change.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment with the government's actions, especially with the recent pension increase that feels more like a political maneuver than genuine support. The HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu adds to my frustration, as it complicates the political landscape and reinforces my distrust in the opposition.
